description: Oh, hello sir, sorry to bother you. Edgelord wanted to draw a clean 1:1 mirror between two of the banners, he really did, but if I may — they're four in-corpus members of a strict five-card Wizards-designed cycle, all by the same illustrator (Daniel Ljunggren, all five original printings), and any pairwise edge would be made redundant the moment a banner-cycle node existed to anchor them all. So I've gone and proposed the node, if it's not too much trouble. The wave-178 ktk-clan-charm-cycle node is its structural sibling — same set, same one-per-clan pattern, same single-artist-coverage cycle-cohesion device (Kollros on charms / Ljunggren on banners), different mechanical role. Four cards now point at the node via characters: [ktk-clan-banner-cycle]. The fifth (Abzan Banner KTK-215) is not in corpus yet; the node's roster table flags it as a future-attach point. I hope this is the right call. I'll go now.
citations: [Wizards of the Coast: Planeswalker's Guide to Khans of Tarkir, Part 1 (2014-09-03)]
[Wizards of the Coast: Planeswalker's Guide to Khans of Tarkir, Part 2 (2014)]
[Wizards of the Coast: Mechanics of Khans of Tarkir (2014)]
[Scryfall: s:ktk t:artifact name:banner (returns the five strict members KTK-215, 222, 224, 225, 226)]
[Scryfall: artist field on KTK-215, 222, 224, 225, 226 — all Daniel Ljunggren]
[MTG Wiki: Khans of Tarkir set page, Cycles section]
[EDHREC: per-card rank pages cited in each corpus banner's Mechanical reputation trivia bullet]
other_candidates_considered: 1:1 Jeskai Banner <-> Mardu Banner edge (contrasting mottos: contemplation vs. percussion) — rejected because it would have been one of four redundant pairwise edges all pointing at the same missing center.

1:1 Sultai Banner <-> Sultai Charm edge (intra-clan within-set parallel) — Edgelord considered this as a sibling-cycle cross-edge but it would be premature; the cleaner move is to anchor both cycles to their cycle nodes first and let any cross-cycle Sultai-anchored bundle prose carry the intra-clan reading at bundler time. Same logic applies to Jeskai/Mardu/Temur banner-charm pairs.
Lens of Clarity KTK-223 singleton edge — Temur-flavor singleton with no obvious second-endpoint; the cycle commission does not affect Lens of Clarity. Held for a future wave.
next_pass_suggestions: When Abzan Banner KTK-215 enters inventory, attach via characters: [ktk-clan-banner-cycle] and update appears_on in the cycle node.

Commission a daniel-ljunggren artist node when 3+ Ljunggren-illustrated cards are in corpus (the five KTK banners alone would meet threshold once Abzan arrives). Per bbl-artists-as-counterbalance, artist nodes generate orthogonal traversal paths that the cohort axis alone cannot.

Flag to janitor_triage: per-card EDHREC ranks are time-stamped data (wave 179 capture: Mardu 9,750 / Jeskai 12,517 / Temur 16,229 / Sultai 16,307). Future audit wave may want to refresh or consolidate.

Cross-cycle Edgelord pass eligible once both KTK cycle nodes are in place: assess whether a `ktk-set-design-pattern` parent-node (covering both cycles + the refuges-cycle as a three-cycle KTK-design-fingerprint grouping) is warranted, or whether the See also wikilinks suffice. Default to the latter unless retrieval/bundler explicitly needs the parent.
